commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The open PR should stay in human maintainer review because the remaining blocker is owner acceptance of protocol/session/security semantics, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security defect was found in the diff; GET and DELETE are bearer-gated and reject browser Origin before auth, while the broader loopback SSE surface remains a maintainer merge-risk decision. (sha=a4e3cedad5a1209e7b69766f1a6838b4dbe05543)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds GET `/mcp` SSE handling, DELETE `/mcp` teardown acknowledgement, stateless POST behavior, and focused gateway tests for the MCP loopback HTTP server.

PR surface: Source +57, Tests +87. Total +144 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main can be reproduced from source by starting the loopback MCP server and sending GET `/mcp`, which reaches the non-POST 405 path despite the generated `type: "http"` Streamable HTTP config.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The open PR should stay in human maintainer review because the remaining blocker is owner acceptance of protocol/session/security semantics, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): support Streamable HTTP MCP transport on loopback server
target.body:
## Summary

The MCP loopback HTTP server generates a config with `"type": "http"` (Streamable HTTP transport), but only handled POST — a GET to `/mcp` returned 405. Claude Code's Streamable HTTP client opens an SSE notification channel by sending GET `/mcp` during initialization; the 405 left it hanging at "still connecting" indefinitely, so the agent got no OpenClaw MCP tools for the whole session.

This adds the missing transport surface, **kept stateless** (the loopback server owns no session lifecycle):

- **GET `/mcp` → 200 `text/event-stream`** — an auth-gated, browser-origin-rejected idle SSE notification channel (the actual fix for the hang). Closes cleanly when the client disconnects.
- **DELETE `/mcp` → 200** — auth-gated no-op acknowledgement of Streamable HTTP session teardown, so clients that send DELETE on close get a clean 200 rather than 405.
- **Stateless POST** — POST responses advertise **no** `Mcp-Session-Id`. The server processes each request independently; advertising a session id it never validates, expires, or owns would be misleading and push clients to echo a session the server ignores. (Per the spec, a stateless server simply omits the session id.)
- `Allow` now advertises `GET, POST, DELETE`; other methods still 405.

## Session semantics

Resolved the stateless-vs-sessionful question in favor of **stateless**: a single-purpose local loopback tool endpoint has no session state to manage, so it stays stateless rather than taking on a session lifecycle. (Maintainer can revisit if sessionful semantics are ever wanted.)

## Tests

`src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts` — added focused gateway transport tests, all passing:
- GET valid bearer → 200 + `text/event-stream`
- GET no auth → 401; GET browser `Origin` → 403
- DELETE valid bearer → 200; DELETE no auth → 401
- unsupported method → 405 with `Allow: GET, POST, DELETE`
- POST stays stateless → no `Mcp-Session-Id` header

## Proof

**Real running loopback MCP server** (built + started from this branch, hit with `curl`):

```
# GET — Streamable HTTP notification channel (the path Claude Code opens during init)
$ curl -i -H "authorization: Bearer <owner-token>" http://127.0.0.1:PORT/mcp
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/event-stream
Cache-Control: no-cache
Connection: keep-alive
# curl then blocks on the open SSE stream (received the ":\n\n" keepalive) — a live
# channel, NOT the old 405 that left Claude Code hanging at "still connecting".

$ curl -i http://127.0.0.1:PORT/mcp                                        # GET, no auth
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized

$ curl -i -H "authorization: Bearer <token>" -H "origin: https://evil.example" .../mcp  # GET, browser origin
HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden

$ curl -i -X DELETE -H "authorization: Bearer <token>" .../mcp             # DELETE teardown
HTTP/1.1 200 OK

$ curl -i -X DELETE http://127.0.0.1:PORT/mcp                              # DELETE, no auth
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized

$ curl -i -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:PORT/mcp                                 # unsupported method
HTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed
Allow: GET, POST, DELETE
```

The GET notification channel now returns `200 + text/event-stream` (a live SSE channel) instead of the `405` that caused the "still connecting" hang — the core fix, demonstrated against a real running server. The same behaviors are pinned by the gateway tests above (real server + `fetch`).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
